stations.name: Morningside railway station
stations.walk_minutes_note: Closest station for southern Balmoral streets.

buses.note: Bus routes serve Balmoral via Riding Road, Bennetts Road, and the Hawthorne Road corridor.

driving.motorway_access: Story Bridge for the CBD. Gateway Motorway via the Cannon Hill interchange.
driving.approx_cbd_minutes_offpeak_note: Approximately 9 to 13 minutes to Brisbane CBD off-peak.

connections: Walking access to Oxford Street Bulimba from many addresses

primary_state.name: Balmoral State School (and overlap with Bulimba SS catchment)

secondary_state.name: Balmoral State High School
secondary_state.note: The state high school is located within the suburb and is the common catchment for many surrounding inner-east addresses.

lifestyle.shops_dining_note: Balmoral itself has limited in-suburb dining. The Oxford Street Bulimba strip and the Hawthorne Garage precinct are within walking distance for many streets. Daily groceries through Bulimba Plaza.

pockets.name: Riverside
pockets.character: Streets within walking distance of the river and the riverwalk. Character queenslanders, premium family-home market.

pockets.name: Bulimba boundary
pockets.character: Streets along the boundary with Bulimba. Often debated as Bulimba or Balmoral by buyers depending on which side of the line.

pockets.name: Hawthorne-adjacent
pockets.character: Southern Balmoral streets bordering Hawthorne. Similar character stock and lifestyle access.

buyer_profile.note: Families targeting the catchment combination and the riverside lifestyle. Largest segment in the suburb.
Buyers moving up from Norman Park or Morningside for river access and the inner-east village strip access.
Long-term inner-east owners moving to smaller character homes or townhouses to remain close to the strip and the river.
